verdict_reason: Both disagreements are substantively explained. The śruti-vipratipannā entry handles the verse's most striking commentarial divergence: Shankara/Prabhupada/Mukundananda all read śruti as 'Veda' (the buddhi tossed about by divergent Vedic teachings); Ramanuja makes a striking lexical move and reads śruti as 'hearing' (specifically Krishna's instruction). The explanation correctly notes both readings preserve the structural claim (closing condition for attaining yoga) while differing on whether the buddhi is being released-from-divergence or grounded-in-instruction. The samādhau acalā buddhiḥ entry correctly preserves the niścalā/acalā distinction (Shankara's two-step gloss vs Ramanuja's eka-vṛtti gloss). Both substantive and named. Pass.
